senses.definition: An old technical term for a substance that can produce acetylene gas when it reacts or breaks down.
examples: The compound was once described as an acetylenogen in early chemical literature.
Heating the mineral releases a gas that was historically called acetylenogen.
Researchers no longer use the word acetylenogen in modern chemistry.

senses.definition: Relating to or containing the acetylenyl group, a chemical group derived from acetylene.
examples: The acetylenyl group reacts readily with halogens.
Acetylenyl derivatives are studied for their electronic properties.
The chemist synthesized an acetylenyl compound for the experiment.

senses.definition: A reactive organic chemical (CH3COF) that is the fluoride form of acetic acid and is used in synthesis.
examples: Acetylfluoride is less common than other acetyl halides in laboratory work.
The reaction produced acetylfluoride as an intermediate.
Special care is needed when handling acetylfluoride due to its reactivity.

senses.definition: A variant or older spelling of acetylglycine, an acetylated form of the amino acid glycine.
examples: The compound was identified as acetylglycin in the early report.
Acetylglycin is formed when glycine is acetylated.
The researcher compared glycine with acetylglycin derivatives.

senses.definition: A chemical made by adding an acetyl group to glycine, used in biochemical and chemical studies.
examples: The enzyme converts glycine into acetylglycine in this pathway.
Acetylglycine can be detected in the urine of some patients.
They synthesized acetylglycine to study its metabolic fate.

senses.definition: An organic compound formed from hydrazine and acetic acid, used as an intermediate in chemical and drug synthesis.
examples: Acetylhydrazine is a known metabolite of some drugs.
The synthesis proceeds through an acetylhydrazine intermediate.
Toxicity studies were carried out on acetylhydrazine.

senses.definition: Relating to acetylene or containing an acetylene group.
examples: The acetylic bond is highly reactive.
Acetylic compounds can undergo addition reactions.
The sample exhibited typical acetylic properties.

senses.definition: A less common spelling of acetylide, a compound where an acetylenic group is bonded to a metal.
examples: The old paper refers to a silver acetylid explosive.
Formation of the acetylid occurs under basic conditions.
The acetylid was isolated as a crystalline solid.

senses.definition: A compound in which an acetylenic carbon group is bonded to a metal, often used as a reactive chemical in synthesis.
examples: Sodium acetylide is used to form new carbon–carbon bonds.
Some copper acetylide compounds are sensitive explosives.
Acetylide ions act as strong nucleophiles in many reactions.

senses.definition: The plural of acetylide, referring to several compounds where an acetylenic group is bonded to metals.
examples: Silver and copper acetylides can be dangerously explosive.
Alkali metal acetylides are important reagents in organic synthesis.
Several acetylides were tested for their catalytic activity.

senses.definition: Another spelling of acetyl iodide, a reactive liquid acid halide (CH3COI) used as a reagent in organic chemistry.
examples: Acetyliodide is less common in the lab than acetyl chloride.
The synthesis makes use of acetyliodide as an acetylating agent.
Acetyliodide hydrolyses easily in the presence of water.

senses.definition: Able to have an acetyl group added in a chemical reaction.
examples: The protein has several acetylizable lysine residues.
Only acetylizable compounds will react under these conditions.
The side chain is easily acetylizable.

senses.definition: The process of introducing an acetyl group into a molecule.
examples: Acetylization of the compound increases its stability.
Enzymes can catalyze the acetylization of proteins.
The reaction mixture showed partial acetylization.

senses.definition: To add an acetyl group to a molecule.
examples: The enzyme can acetylize several different substrates.
Chemists acetylize the alcohol to protect it during the reaction.
Under these conditions, the reagent will acetylize the amine group.

senses.definition: Having had an acetyl group added to it.
examples: The acetylized form of the drug is more active.
Acetylized proteins can show different functions.
They measured levels of acetylized histones in the cells.

senses.definition: A reagent, device, or enzyme that causes acetylation.
examples: The enzyme acts as an acetylizer in this pathway.
They added an acetylizer to start the reaction.
The acetylizer converts the substrate into its acetylated form.

senses.definition: Causing or involving the addition of an acetyl group.
examples: The acetylizing enzyme modifies the protein after translation.
Acetylizing conditions were applied to the reaction mixture.
They studied the effect of acetylizing agents on cell growth.

senses.definition: Present participle of acetylize: adding an acetyl group to something.
examples: The enzyme is acetylizing the peptide at several sites.
By acetylizing the molecule, they changed its solubility.

senses.definition: An organic compound, also called acetoin, produced by some bacteria during fermentation.
examples: The presence of acetylmethylcarbinol is detected by the Voges–Proskauer test.
Acetylmethylcarbinol accumulates in certain fermentation processes.
The bacteria produced large amounts of acetylmethylcarbinol.

senses.definition: A reactive and often unstable peroxide made from acetyl groups, used as an initiator in polymerization.
examples: Acetylperoxide can act as a radical initiator.
Because acetylperoxide is shock sensitive, it must be handled carefully.
The polymerization was started with acetylperoxide.

senses.definition: Any phenol molecule that has an acetyl group attached to it.
examples: The researcher synthesized an acetylphenol derivative.
Acetylphenol compounds show different reactivity from phenol.
They measured the toxicity of several acetylphenols.

senses.definition: An organic compound formed by acetylating phenylhydrazine, used as a reagent and in research.
examples: Acetylphenylhydrazine is used to induce anemia in laboratory animals.
The synthesis produces acetylphenylhydrazine as an intermediate.
Special precautions are required when handling acetylphenylhydrazine.

senses.definition: A dye made by acetylating rosaniline, related to some early synthetic dyes.
examples: Acetylrosaniline was examined as a textile dye.
The color of acetylrosaniline changes with pH.
The patent described a process for producing acetylrosaniline.

senses.definition: Plural of acetyl, referring to several acetyl groups or acetyl derivatives.
examples: The molecule contains three acetyls attached to the ring.
Different acetyls can give the compound new properties.
They compared the activity of various acetyls of the drug.

senses.definition: A salt or ester of acetylsalicylic acid, often related to aspirin or its derivatives.
examples: Calcium acetylsalicylate is better tolerated by some patients.
They measured blood levels of acetylsalicylate after dosing.
The drug contains an acetylsalicylate as its active ingredient.

senses.definition: Plural of acetylsalicylate, referring to several salts or esters of acetylsalicylic acid.
examples: Different acetylsalicylates have different solubilities.
The study compared several acetylsalicylates of the drug.
Acetylsalicylates are widely used as pain relievers.

senses.definition: Relating to acetylsalicylic acid, the chemical name for aspirin.
examples: Acetylsalicylic acid is commonly used to treat pain and fever.
The acetylsalicylic compound showed anti-inflammatory effects.
They studied the metabolism of acetylsalicylic derivatives.

senses.definition: An older pharmaceutical compound made from acetylsalicylic acid and phenyl salicylate, used in the past as an analgesic.
examples: Acetylsalol was once prescribed for digestive problems.
Modern medicine has largely replaced acetylsalol with newer drugs.
Early reports describe acetylsalol as a mild antiseptic.

senses.definition: A tannin that has been chemically modified by adding acetyl groups.
examples: Acetyltannin is used in some medicinal preparations.
The acetyltannin showed lower astringency than the original tannin.
They isolated acetyltannin from the plant extract.

senses.definition: A derivative of thymol in which an acetyl group has been added, sometimes used for its antiseptic properties.
examples: Acetylthymol was evaluated as a potential antiseptic.
The essential oil contained small amounts of acetylthymol.
They synthesized acetylthymol to improve thymol's stability.

senses.definition: A derivative of the alkaloid tropeine that has been acetylated, historically explored for medical use.
examples: Acetyltropeine was tested as a potential antispasmodic.
The pharmacological properties of acetyltropeine were described in early studies.
Few modern drugs are based directly on acetyltropeine.

senses.definition: A compound formed by combining an acetyl group with urea.
examples: Acetylurea can crystallize from the reaction mixture.
The researcher prepared acetylurea to study its properties.
Acetylurea derivatives have been mentioned in older patents.

senses.definition: Written short form of acetylcholine, a chemical that carries signals between nerve cells.
examples: Blocking ACh at the synapse can affect muscle control.
The drug increases ACh release in the brain.
Low ACh levels are linked with some memory problems.

senses.definition: A sound used in writing to show a little pain, effort, or annoyance, especially in some European languages.
examples: “Ach, I forgot my keys again,” he muttered.
“Ach, this box is heavy,” she said as she lifted it.
“Ach, what a day,” he sighed.

senses.definition: Relating to the ancient Greek people of Achaea or to their culture and language.
examples: The poet wrote about the Achaean heroes of the Trojan War.
Achaean dialects influenced later forms of Greek.
The museum displays Achaean pottery and tools.

senses.definition: A person from ancient Achaea in Greece.
examples: The Achaeans sailed to Troy according to the legend.
As an Achaean, he was bound by strict codes of honor.
The poet calls the Greek warriors Achaeans.

senses.definition: Relating to the Achaemenid dynasty or empire of ancient Persia.
examples: The Achaemenian kings ruled a vast empire.
Achaemenian art is known for its detailed reliefs.
Many Achaemenian sites have been excavated in Iran.

senses.definition: Relating to the Achaemenid dynasty, the first great Persian empire, or its culture and art.
examples: Achaemenid architecture blended many regional styles.
The Achaemenid period saw major road-building projects.
Achaemenid reliefs often show royal processions.

senses.definition: A member of the Achaemenid royal family or someone from the Achaemenid empire.
examples: The Achaemenids controlled lands from Egypt to India.
As an Achaemenid, he belonged to the ruling elite.
The Achaemenids left many royal inscriptions.

senses.definition: A Latinized plural name for the family or line of Achaemenid rulers of ancient Persia.
examples: The Achaemenidae established one of the largest empires of antiquity.
Historians debate how the Achaemenidae managed such a diverse realm.
Many inscriptions praise the power of the Achaemenidae.

senses.definition: Another form of Achaemenid, relating to the Achaemenid dynasty or empire.
examples: Achaemenidian inscriptions have been found at Persepolis.
The site contains typical Achaemenidian columns.
Achaemenidian reliefs show royal ceremonies.

senses.definition: A group or collection of achenes, which are small dry one-seeded fruits that do not open when ripe.
examples: The flower head bears numerous achaenia.
Microscopic study of the achaenia revealed fine surface patterns.
The plant disperses its achaenia by wind.

senses.definition: A variant spelling of achene, a small dry fruit with one seed that does not open when ripe.
examples: Each achaenium contains a single seed.
The botanist measured the length of each achaenium.
The wind carried the light achaenia and each achaenium far from the parent plant.

senses.definition: Plural of achaenium, meaning several small dry one-seeded fruits that do not open when ripe.
examples: The plant produces many achaeniums in late summer.
They examined the shape of the achaeniums under a microscope.
The achaeniums fall to the ground and germinate the next year.

senses.definition: A plant whose fruits are achenes, or a fruiting structure that bears achenes.
examples: The species is described as an achaenocarp in older classifications.
The achaenocarp bears numerous dry fruits.
Botanists compared the achaenocarp with fleshy-fruited species.

senses.definition: Plural of achaenocarp, referring to plants or fruiting bodies that produce achenes.
examples: Several achaenocarps were grouped together in the key.
These achaenocarps dominate the dry grassland.
The field survey recorded many achaenocarps among the flora.

senses.definition: The name of a genus of extinct mammals known from fossils.
examples: Achaenodon is known from Eocene fossil deposits.
The skull of Achaenodon shows specialized teeth.
Paleontologists described Achaenodon as a large ungulate-like mammal.

senses.definition: Having no bristles or setae, especially used of worms and similar animals.
examples: Some species are achaeta in the adult stage.
The worm was described as achaeta due to the lack of bristles.
Achaeta forms contrast with species that have well-developed setae.

senses.definition: Without setae or bristles, especially in worms or insect larvae.
examples: The larva is achaetous and smooth-bodied.
Achaetous forms are less common in this family of worms.
The species is described as an achaetous annelid.

senses.definition: To cause to become irritated, angry, or emotionally sore.
examples: The unfair remarks began to achafe him.
She was achafed by their constant criticism.
The delay achafed the waiting crowd.

senses.definition: A small payment or tax once charged for using weights and measures, in old English law.
examples: Merchants were required to pay achage at the market.
The record lists the amount of achage collected that year.
Achage was one of several minor medieval dues.

senses.definition: Plural of achage, referring to several such fees or instances of this old tax.
examples: The charter listed rents, fines, and achages.
Various achages were imposed on traders.
The lord reduced the achages to encourage commerce.

senses.definition: The name of an Indigenous people of Colombia and Venezuela and of their language.
examples: The Achagua traditionally lived along the Orinoco plains.
Linguists have documented the Achagua language.
Achagua communities continue to maintain their cultural practices.

senses.definition: The name of a Pashtun tribe mainly living in parts of Afghanistan and Pakistan, and a family name from that group.
examples: The Achakzai are one of the Pashtun tribes of the region.
Several politicians come from the Achakzai tribe.
He proudly identified himself as an Achakzai.
